The Electrical and Electronics Engineering Research & Project Laboratory provides a working environment where engineering students can develop innovative projects by transforming their theoretical knowledge into practice. Our laboratory provides all the necessary infrastructure for testing, researching and prototyping current technologies in electrical and electronics engineering.

In our laboratory, students and researchers can carry out projects on various topics such as circuit design, microcontrollers, sensors, power systems, communication systems, robotics and automation. Our laboratory is continuously updated to provide both theoretical and applied engineering knowledge.

The Electrical and Electronics Engineering Research & Project Laboratory encourages interdisciplinary collaborations, allowing students to develop creative and original projects. In addition, it collaborates with the industry to produce solutions for the needs of the engineering world and contributes to scientific research activities.

Our laboratory is of great importance to host projects that will contribute to the engineering world, develop students' innovation skills and train the engineers of the future.
